source: TwinBERT- Distilling Knowledge to Twin-Structured BERT Models for Efficient Retrieval

concepts.finding: TwinBERT: lightweight dual-encoder with two BERT sub-encoders (query, document) sharing weights. Knowledge distillation from cross-attention BERT teacher. Key techniques: (1) layer reduction (4-6 layers vs 12), (2) shared weights across towers, (3) late interaction after pooling. Achieves 10-50x speedup over cross-attention with <3% accuracy loss.
concepts.implication: For keyword-intent production systems: lightweight dual-encoder with shared weights is efficient. Knowledge distillation from a more powerful (cross-attention) model improves quality. Late interaction (pooled embeddings) is faster than full cross-attention.
concepts.open_question: What is the minimum encoder size for effective keyword-intent matching? Is there a practical accuracy-speed tradeoff curve?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A TwinBERT-style lightweight keyword encoder (4-layer transformer, dual-tower with shared weights) achieves 95%+ of full BERT keyword-intent accuracy at 10x lower latency.
